file.content:
set -g default-terminal "tmux-256color"
set -ga terminal-overrides ",xterm-ghostty:RGB"

set -g prefix C-s
unbind C-b
bind C-z send-prefix

unbind %
bind | split-window -h

unbind '"'
bind - split-window -v

unbind r
bind r source-file ~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf

set -g mouse on

bind -r j resize-pane -D 5
bind -r k resize-pane -U 5
bind -r h resize-pane -L 5
bind -r l resize-pane -R 5

# start numbering at 1
set -g base-index 1
setw -g pane-base-index 1

# set status bar black with opacity

set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tpm'
set -g @plugin 'niksingh710/minimal-tmux-status'
set -g @minimal-tmux-indicator-str "[T]"
set -g @minimal-tmux-status "top"
set -g @minimal-tmux-show-expanded-icons-for-all-tabs true
set -g @minimal-tmux-status-right "[T]"
set -g @minimal-tmux-justify "right"
run '~/.config/tmux/plugins/tpm/tpm'

set -g status-right " "

set -g mode-style "bg=#5f87af,fg=#ffffff,bold"
setw -g mode-keys vi
bind -T copy-mode-vi Escape send -X cancel
bind -T copy-mode-vi v send -X begin-selection
bind -T copy-mode-vi C-v send -X rectangle-toggle
bind -T copy-mode-vi y send -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "pbcopy"

content.text: Two parts: Ghostty needs to forward `cmd+k` as `ctrl+k` to the PTY, then tmux intercepts it and clears screen + scrollback.

input.new_string:
bind -n C-k send-keys C-l \; clear-history

bind -T copy-mode-vi Escape send -X cancel

content.text:
Reload tmux config: `prefix + r` (your `C-s r`), then restart Ghostty.

How it works:
- Ghostty intercepts `cmd+k` and sends `\x0b` (Ctrl+K) to the PTY
- tmux catches `C-k` (no prefix needed), sends `C-l` to clear visible screen, then `clear-history` to wipe the scrollback buffer

data.command: case "$TERM_PROGRAM" in ghostty) B='com.mitchellh.ghostty';; iTerm.app) B='com.googlecode.iterm2';; WarpTerminal) B='dev.warp.Warp-Stable';; Apple_Terminal) B='com.apple.Terminal';; *) B='';; esac; terminal-notifier -title 'Claude Code' -message 'Claude finished' -sound 'Glass' ${B:+-activate "$B"} 2>/dev/null || true

content.text: Wrong action name. Ghostty uses `text` not `write_to_pty`.

toolUseResult: Error: Ripgrep search timed out after 20 seconds. The search may have matched files but did not complete in time. Try searching a more specific path or pattern.

content.text:
The real issue: Ghostty has its own scrollback buffer. `tmux clear-history` only clears tmux's buffer — Ghostty's buffer remains.

Fix: send `\e[3J` (erase-scrollback escape sequence) so Ghostty ALSO clears its buffer.

input.new_string: bind -n C-k send-keys 'printf "\e[3J" && clear && tmux clear-history' Enter

lines: +bind -n C-k send-keys 'printf "\e[3J" && clear && tmux clear-history' Enter

content.text:
Reload tmux config (`C-s r`). Now Cmd+K will:
1. `\e[3J` — clears Ghostty's scrollback buffer
2. `clear` — clears visible screen
3. `tmux clear-history` — clears tmux's buffer
